sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Looking at the image, I can see red and blue are now both at cols 18-21 (next to each other), and green is below them. Let me check exact positions.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check positions after retract
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    if n['color'] in ('R', 'b', 'N', 'M'):
        boundary = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in boundary]
        cols = [p[1] for p in boundary]
        if min(rows) < 50:
            print(f"{n['color']}: rows={min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols={min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Current positions:
- Player: rows 30-35, cols 11-16
- Red: rows 31-34, cols 18-21
- Blue: rows 31-34, cols 24-27 (NOT at cols 18-21!)
- Green: rows 37-40, cols 18-21
Interesting! When I retracted, red returned to cols 18-21, but blue stayed at cols 24-27 (not 18-21). This suggests that when multiple blocks are collected, retracting only returns the LAST collected block to cols 18-21, and the other blocks stay at intermediate positions.
Wait, but earlier when I collected red and blue together, red was at cols 36-39 and blue was at cols 42-45. After retracting, red is at cols 18-21 and blue is at cols 24-27. So red returned all the way to cols 18-21, but blue only moved from cols 42-45 to cols 24-27.
Hmm, this is confusing. Let me think about the order. When I extended RIGHT, I first collected red (at cols 18-21) and then blue (at cols 42-45). So red was collected first, blue second. When I retract, the last collected block (blue) should return to cols 18-21, but instead red returned.
Actually, maybe the retract operation returns blocks in reverse order of collection. So blue (collected last) returns first, but it only moves to cols 24-27 because red is already at cols 18-21 and blocks can't overlap.
Wait, but red is at cols 18-21 and blue is at cols 24-27. They're at the same row (31-34) but different columns. So they don't overlap. Why didn't blue return to cols 18-21?
Maybe the retract operation only returns ONE block at a time. I need to retract again to return the next block.
Let me try retracting again.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try retracting again to return blue to cols 18-21
action(['LEFT'] * 5)
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
